description: Add an HTML font-size override option to the plugin settings. Bricks sometimes forces html { font-size: 62.5% } regardless of user settings. The plugin should offer an option to force the html font-size to 100% or 62.5%, or leave it alone (default).
steps: Step 1: Add a new WordPress option key. Use the existing 'slashed_bricks_tokens' option with a new top-level section 'general' (or store it as a separate option 'slashed_bricks_html_font_size'). Given the existing architecture stores everything in 'slashed_bricks_tokens', add a new section. However, since this is not a token but a plugin behavior setting, use a separate option: 'slashed_bricks_settings' with key 'html_font_size' having values '', '100', or '62.5'. Store as a separate option to keep token overrides and plugin settings cleanly separated.

Step 2: Add the font-size setting UI to the legacy admin page (class-admin-page.php). Add a new section at the top of render_page() (before the tab navigation) or as a new 'General' tab. Since the existing tabs are all token sections and this is a plugin-behavior setting, add it as a settings panel above the tabs. Render a 'Plugin Settings' section with a <select> for HTML Font Size with three options: 'Default (don't override)' (value ''), 'Force 100%' (value '100'), 'Force 62.5%' (value '62.5'). Handle save/read via the existing form submission mechanism but targeting the new option.
Step 3: Update handle_save() in class-admin-page.php to also save the html_font_size setting from the form POST data into the separate 'slashed_bricks_settings' option.

Step 4: Update class-enqueue.php to inject the font-size CSS override. In enqueue_frontend_styles(), after the token override injection, check the 'slashed_bricks_settings' option for 'html_font_size'. If it is '100' or '62.5', inject `html { font-size: 100% !important; }` or `html { font-size: 62.5% !important; }` via wp_add_inline_style() attached to the 'slashed-framework' handle. This ensures the override loads on both frontend and the Bricks canvas iframe (same contexts where the framework CSS loads).

Step 5: Expose the setting in the Svelte admin page. Update the wp_localize_script hydration in class-admin-page-svelte.php to include the 'slashed_bricks_settings' option in the bootstrap payload. Add a REST endpoint or extend the existing one to handle saving the general settings. Since the REST controller currently only handles token sections, add a new route '/settings' (POST) that saves to 'slashed_bricks_settings'.

Step 6: Update the REST controller (class-rest-controller.php) to add a new route: POST /wp-json/slashed-bricks/v1/settings that accepts { html_font_size: '' | '100' | '62.5' } and saves to 'slashed_bricks_settings'. Add a GET route as well for reading.

Step 7: Update the Svelte admin SPA to expose the font-size setting. This is lower priority - the legacy admin page is the primary UI. At minimum, pass the setting through in the hydration payload so the Svelte app has access to it for future development.
acceptance_criteria: A new option 'slashed_bricks_settings' is used to store the html_font_size value
The legacy admin page shows a font-size selector with three options: Default, Force 100%, Force 62.5%
Saving the setting persists it to the database via the existing form handler
class-enqueue.php reads the setting and injects appropriate inline CSS when the value is non-empty
The inline CSS uses !important to override Bricks' forced font-size
The REST controller has a /settings endpoint for the Svelte SPA
All PHP files pass syntax check (php -l)
verification: Run `php -l integrations/bricks/includes/class-admin-page.php` and confirm no syntax errors
Run `php -l integrations/bricks/includes/class-enqueue.php` and confirm no syntax errors
Run `php -l integrations/bricks/includes/class-rest-controller.php` and confirm no syntax errors
Run `php -l integrations/bricks/includes/class-admin-page-svelte.php` and confirm no syntax errors
Run `php -l integrations/bricks/slashed-bricks.php` and confirm no syntax errors
